title: Quiz: Degradable and Anti-Degradable Channels
description: Test your understanding of channel degradability

questions.question: A channel N is degradable if:

options.text: N = D o N^c for some CPTP map D
N^c = D o N for some CPTP map D

questions.explanation: A degradable channel has N^c = D o N, meaning the receiver can simulate the environment's view.

questions.question: For a degradable channel, the quantum capacity Q equals:
questions.explanation: For degradable channels, Q = max I_c without regularization - the capacity has a single-letter formula.

questions.question: Anti-degradable channels always have zero quantum capacity.
questions.explanation: True. For anti-degradable channels, the environment can simulate the receiver, so I_c <= 0 always, giving Q = 0.

questions.question: The amplitude damping channel with gamma = 0.3 is:
questions.explanation: Amplitude damping is degradable for gamma <= 0.5, and gamma = 0.3 < 0.5.

questions.question: The complementary channel N^c describes:

options.text: What the environment learns about the input

questions.explanation: The complementary channel gives the state of the environment after the interaction, describing what information leaks to the environment.

questions.question: The erasure channel is:
questions.explanation: The erasure channel is degradable for all erasure probabilities p, making its quantum capacity easy to compute.

questions.question: Entanglement-breaking channels are always anti-degradable.
questions.explanation: True. Entanglement-breaking channels destroy all entanglement, meaning the environment effectively captures all quantum information, making them anti-degradable with Q = 0.

questions.question: At gamma = 0.5, the amplitude damping channel is:
questions.explanation: At gamma = 0.5, amplitude damping is self-complementary: the channel equals its complement. This is the boundary between degradable and anti-degradable.
